messages.content:
I can’t run tasks from here, but I can tell you exactly how to run “load test task 1” locally or in CI — I just need one detail: which test framework / where the task is defined (npm script, Makefile, k6, Locust, Artillery, Shipyard task, GitHub Action, Docker, etc.). 

Quick guidance for the most common cases (pick the one that matches your repo):

- npm script
  - Check package.json for the script name (look for "task1" or "load:test:task1").
  - Run: npm install && npm run <script-name>

- Makefile
  - Run: make task1

- k6
  - Typical command: k6 run tests/load_test_task1.js
  - With environment and settings: TARGET=https://example.com k6 run --vus 100 --duration 1m tests/load_test_task1.js
  - Docker: docker run --rm -v $(pwd):/scripts -w /scripts loadimpact/k6 run tests/load_test_task1.js

- Locust (Python)
  - Headless run: locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 1m --host https://example.com

- Artillery
  - Run: artillery run tests/task1.yml

- Gatling / Maven
  - Example: mvn -Dgatling.simulationClass=com.example.Task1Simulation gatling:test

What you should set up before running
- Install the tool (k6, locust, artillery, Node/Python etc.)
- Ensure required environment variables (TARGET_URL, API keys) are set
- Confirm test configuration (vus, duration, ramp-up) matches your goals
- If the test hits a live system, coordinate with stakeholders to avoid accidental outages

How to capture results
- k6: use --summary-export=out.json or --out influxdb
- Locust: check summary printed to console or use --html option if available
- Artillery: produces a summary and JSON output with --output

Next step
- Tell me which framework/task file you have (or paste the task definition or the relevant package.json/Makefile/shipyard config), and I’ll give the exact command and any recommended parameters and thresholds.

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
